jobs.prompt: Create one clean academic paper figure with one shared DirectNet pipeline: log-power spectrogram -> RehearFitEncoder -> FC1/FC2 -> FC3 -> 12-band LTASS output. Do not draw two full pipelines. Put a large zoom callout around FC3. Inside the zoom, show only the initialization difference with two compact alternatives: random init has random FC3 bias and weights and must discover the baseline curve; mean init has FC3 weights = 0 and FC3 bias = train-subset mean curve, so it starts near the LTASS prior. Keep the rest of the flow identical and visually muted. Bottom short labels: same encoder, same augmentation, same target, different FC3 initialization. White background, vector schematic, teal blue orange accents, sparse readable labels.

Create one clean academic paper figure with a single shared model flow: spectrogram -> RehearFitEncoder -> FC head -> LTASS prediction. Emphasize the concept with a large central formula-style visual at FC3: prediction = train mean + learned residual. Show random init as a small warning inset where the model must learn mean plus residual from scratch. Show mean initialization as the main highlighted route: FC3 bias stores the train-subset mean curve and the network learns only residual correction. Do not split into two separate pipelines. Use curve icons instead of text paragraphs. White background, crisp vector linework, paper figure style, sparse labels.

Create one clean academic concept figure using one shared DirectNet pipeline: input spectrogram -> RehearFitEncoder -> FC head -> LTASS output. Enlarge the FC3 initialization block and connect it to two small output-curve sketches: random FC3 starts far from the population LTASS curve and requires large correction; train-mean FC3 starts on the train mean curve and only needs small residual adjustment. The visual message should be why mean initialization helps low-label training. Keep all other parts of the model shown once, shared, and simple. Use short labels: random start, mean start, large correction, small residual. White background, clean vector ML schematic, restrained teal blue orange palette.
